LHX.sentence: L3Harris barely moved today, but its profit margins are running well below its bigger defense rivals, and the open question is whether that's a one-time hit or something more lasting.

ASML.sentence: ASML ticked up nicely, which is business as usual for the only company in the world that makes the machines needed to print the most advanced computer chips.

LIN.sentence: Linde had a slightly down day, but the industrial gas business is as steady as ever, with long-term hospital and factory contracts quietly humming along.

MSFT.sentence: Microsoft has drifted down and is now trading well below its recent high, which is unusual for them given the AI cloud business keeps humming — it looks cheaper than it has in a while.

TMO.sentence: Thermo Fisher is soft this stretch because labs have been working through backlogs of supplies, but that slowdown appears to be ending and growth should pick back up.

ENB.sentence: Enbridge dipped a touch, and while these oil and gas pipelines earn steady tolls, the stock looks a bit expensive relative to what it's paying out.

NOW.sentence: ServiceNow is up a bit today but sits far below its peak — a striking drop for a company whose workflow-automation and AI tools still get snapped up by big corporations and the government.

GLNCY.sentence: Glencore edged up as copper prices have been surging, and this miner-and-trader tends to catch up to copper after a lag, so it may have some room to follow.

IBIT.sentence: Bitcoin bounced today but is still well below where it's traded on average over the past year, so it's been in a cooler stretch after earlier highs.

KOF.sentence: The Coca-Cola bottler for Latin America is quiet, with the Mexican peso steady — nothing much to report here.

PBR.A.sentence: Petrobras rose a bit and its dividends look generous right now, but oil profits can be at their best just before they turn, so there's some risk the good stretch doesn't last.

AMKBY.sentence: Maersk jumped today, a welcome move for the Danish container-shipping giant, though its stock is still priced below the value of the ships and assets it owns.
